summary: No-ship: pagination is subprocess-bounded but still unbounded in Bun heap and output, preserving the fork-saturation suppression path.

findings.title: Blocker: exhaustive pagination is re-bounded by the parent process
findings.body: `fetchConnection` retains every full attacker-controlled page in `pages` and parses them only after enumeration. The normalized records are subsequently retained and serialized too. Same-head fork PRs can therefore grow Bun heap and workflow output linearly until the executor is killed or logs are exhausted, suppressing polling every cycle. This is new evidence beyond R-33: the accepted design said “page → reduce → cursor,” but the implementation performs “page → retain all → parse/output all.”
findings.recommendation: Fold and validate each page immediately, retaining only decision-relevant trusted/same-repository facts plus bounded counters. Discard raw pages and emit bounded diagnostics; do not introduce a page cap.

findings.title: Test-quality: W70 pins the vulnerable representation instead of public behavior
findings.body: The meaningful contract is already covered by finding PR #382 on the last page and reaching skip with no push/create. Requiring `.observed.prs` to contain all 651 nodes additionally freezes retention and serialization of every untrusted fork. This wrong-seam assertion makes the bounded streaming design fail and forces future work to choose between preserving the resource-exhaustion surface or editing a locked regression.
findings.recommendation: Rebuild the RED baseline with the same saturated input and end-to-end behavior, but assert a bounded total-count summary and last-page trusted result instead of the full node array. Keep that revised contract byte-identical across RED→GREEN.

next_steps: Implement a streaming page reducer with bounded result/output state.
Re-freeze the corrected saturation witness before applying the production fix.
Regenerate the RED/GREEN lock and verification records, then rerun the structure gate.
